Each* *SKU has multiple sub-components and there are multiple SKUs which require different machining and/or assembly processes. The* *time for machining and or assembly for each SKU is different. The capacity utilisation also depends on the SKU’s that we* *manufacture as per demand. Hence, capacity utilization in the table above has been computed based on the number of available* *hours of machines used in the manufacturing processes during a period (“*   * **Available Capacity** *   *”) and the number of hours for which* *such machines were utilized during the relevant period.* *(2)*    *The calculation of the hours for which machines were available during the periods included above is based on the following* *assumptions and estimates: (i) each of the machines operates for 22.5 hours a day (after deducting 30 minutes due for shift change)* *across 302 working days in a financial year (excluding one weekly holiday) (or 227 working days during the nine months ended* *September 30, 2025): (ii) in case of heat treatment plant at Thirumudivakkam which is continuous process plant, the Available* *Capacity is computed on 342 days (excluding the mandatory maintenance period); and (iii) the manufacturing facilities located in* *Thirumudivakkam, Tamil Nadu and Talegaon, Maharashtra operate on a 3 shift basis and the manufacturing facility located in* *Mannur, Tamil Nadu operates on a 2 shift basis in the financial years ended December 31, 2022 and December 31, 2023 and one* *shift basis in the financial year ended December 31, 2024 and the nine months period ended September 30, 2025.* *(3)*    *In case of the manufacturing facility located in Mannur, Tamil Nadu, certain equipment have been relocated to the assembly plant* *located at Talegaon, Maharashtra in the financial years ended December 31, 2023 and December 31, 2024 due to business* *requirement. Hence, the same is reflected in the reduced available capacity for the respective periods in Mannur, Tamil Nadu* *facility. The Mannur plant operated for 2 shifts basis in the financial years ended December 31, 2022 and December 31, 2023 and* *one shift basis in the financial year ended December 31, 2024 and the nine months period ended September 30, 2025. The Talegaon* *plant operated on a 3 shifts basis throughout the years. Owing to these shifts, a direct year-on-year installed capacity is not* *comparable.* *(4)*    *In case of the machine shop at the manufacturing facility located in Thirumudivakkam, Tamil Nadu, certain machines have been* *added in the financial years ended December 31, 2023 and December 31, 2024, resulting in an increase in the Available Capacity.*

Chunk 78
----------------------------------------
## **INTERNAL RISKS**

If we fail to accurately forecast order volumes or plan production schedules, we may experience capacity utilisation inefficiencies that could impact our operational performance and financial results. Since a portion of our gearboxes and drive systems are manufactured for direct customers exclusively on order, our production planning relies on demand forecasting rather than inventory management to that extent. Timing mismatches between production planning cycles and fluctuating order patterns may create operational disruptions affecting manufacturing schedules, increasing costs, delaying deliveries, and potentially damaging our market reputation, thereby adversely affecting our business, financial condition, results of operations, and cash flows.

40
* **11.** *    * **Our ongoing and proposed capacity addition plans are subject to the risk of unanticipated delays in** * * **implementation and cost overruns.** *
We have made and intend to continue making investments to add capacities through additional manufacturing facilities close to our existing facility in Maharashtra to aid our growth efforts. We have recently commenced manufacturing electric motors at our facility in Mannur (Tamil Nadu) in October 2025 and intend to establish a new manufacturing facility in Talegaon (Maharashtra), adjacent to our existing operational facility to focus on manufacturing heavy-duty gearboxes. The land in Talegaon (Maharashtra) on which we propose to establish our new facility is currently held by one of our Group Companies, Bonfiglioli Drive Solutions Private Limited (“  **BDSPL**  ”), under lease agreement entered into by BDSPL with MIDC. We are in the process of acquiring the leasehold rights from BDSPL which remains subject to approvals from MIDC and there can be no assurance that the leasehold will be transferred from BDSPL to us within the anticipated timelines or at all.
Our expansion plans remain subject to the potential problems and uncertainties that construction projects face including cost overruns or delays. Problems that could adversely affect our expansion plans include labour shortages, increased costs of equipment or manpower, delays or defects in design or construction, the possibility of unanticipated future regulatory restrictions, incremental preoperative expenses, taxes and duties, interest and finance charges, working capital margin, environment and ecology costs and other external factors which may not be within the control of our management. We may also face interruptions in the supply of electricity and water required for our construction efforts and operations. Further, there might be price fluctuations and the currently estimated project costs may increase on account of factors beyond our control, including increase in cost of machinery and associated transportation or other charges or taxes. Failure to complete the development on time and within budget could adversely affect our business, financial condition, results of operations, and cash flows.

Chunk 79
----------------------------------------
## **INTERNAL RISKS**

Under Indian laws, manufacturing facilities such as ours are subject to government approvals and permits, including registration and licences under the Factories Act,1948 environment protection approvals, pollution discharge permits, drainage licences, work safety approvals, labour related approvals, fire protection approvals, among others. See “  * **Key Regulations and Policies in India** *  ” and “  * **Government and Other Approvals** *  ” on pages 219 and 366, respectively. There is no assurance that we will be able to comply with the requirements of such government supervision or procure the approvals in a timely manner or at all. As a result, we may be subject to administrative uncertainty, fines, or the delays of use of such projects. Any of the foregoing could materially and adversely affect our business operations. See also “  * **-  We require certain approvals and licences in the ordinary** * * **course of business, and the failure to obtain or retain them in a timely manner may adversely affect our** * * **operations** *  ” on page 45.

Chunk 80
----------------------------------------
## **INTERNAL RISKS**

*(1)*    *Inter-company sales includes sale of finished goods such as yaw drives and swing drives to related parties for onward sale to their*

41
*customers.* *(2)*    *Others includes non-core sales such as the sale of components to vendors.*
We enter into agreements with our dealers for one-year terms that are automatically renewed yearly, and as a result, have long-standing relationships with most of our dealers. As of September 30, 2025, 16 out of 44 dealers had been engaged with us for 20 years or more. We are therefore dependent on our existing dealer network to the extent of the revenue we generate from sales to the network. Our dealers are incentivized through discounts they are entitled to claim depending on the sales they make. Our dealers’ continued association with us is dependent to a large extent on our brand, price competitiveness, efficient and timely product deliveries, and consistent product quality, all of which impact the marketability of our products and therefore volume of sales by dealers. If we are unable to provide adequate gearboxes to meet the demand of our dealers for sales or after-sales services due to insufficient manufacturing capacity, miscommunications, inaccurate demand forecast or otherwise, our sales and after-sale services could be negatively affected. In addition, some of our dealers are not engaged with us on an exclusive basis and have the ability to distribute products of our competitors in India. If our relationship with a dealer is terminated or if a dealer prefers to focus on its business with our competitors and reduces purchases of our gearboxes, we may not be able to replace such relationship with a new dealer in a timely manner or at all. In addition, any such new relationship may take time to develop and may not be as favourable to us as the relationship it is replacing.
Furthermore, since dealers have direct contact with our customers when they purchase our gearboxes and during the after-sale services, their conduct has a significant impact on the customers’ perception of our gearboxes and our brand. Our dealer network may also be affected by issues affecting dealers such as bankruptcy, non- compliance of legal requirements, failure of their information systems or any security breach or any labour unrest and slowdowns, and the adverse effects of the seasonality of sales, could lead to fluctuations in our revenue and could also negatively affect both, our financial performance and our reputation in the industry and amongst customers. For instance, we have previously had some dealers default on their payment obligations towards us for an amount of ₹5.69 million in Fiscal 2017. We have since initiated legal proceedings against such dealers to recover the sum owed to us. There can be no assurance that the legal proceedings will be decided in our favour or that we will not experience similar issues with our existing dealer network in the future. Any such disputes in the future or any failure to renew our existing arrangements with our dealer network on similar terms or at all, could impact our ability to access end-customers across a wide range of industries, which could adversely affect our business, financial condition, results of operations, and cash flows.

## **INTERNAL RISKS**

42
Our global sales are subject us to risks inherent in doing business in such geographies. Some of these risks include, (i) the unexpected deterioration of the relationship between India and countries to which we export our products to or from which we source parts and materials, including the imposition of trade and economic sanctions and the revocation of free trade agreements; (ii) the failure to comply with a variety of international, national and local legal and regulatory requirements, including consumer protection, data protection, intellectual property, tax and trade law, as well as tariffs, export quotas, customs duties or other trade restrictions; (iii) the potential for unexpected changes in legal, political, regulatory, social and economic conditions or policies in the countries in which our products are sold and in countries from which we source supplies from third-party suppliers; (iv) exposure to liabilities under various anti-corruption and anti-money laundering laws; (v) the need to adjust our business approach effectively to target customers in the various countries in which we sell our products, and adapt our product offering and our logistics, payment, fulfilment and customer care practices to take account of local preferences, customs and practices; and (vi) increasing rates of inflation and foreign exchange fluctuations, which would affect our profitability. The occurrence of any of the risks described above could have a material adverse effect on our business, financial condition, results of operations, cash flows and prospects. While none of these risks has materialized or had an adverse impact on our sales and operations in the nine months period ended September 30, 2025 and Fiscals 2024, 2023, 2022, any significant changes to global trade policies, particularly in respect of tariffs that may be imposed for imports into the United States from India, may materially impact our business, financial condition, results of operations, and cash flows.

## **INTERNAL RISKS**

Involvement in such proceedings and any ongoing investigations by regulatory or tax authorities, could divert our management’s time and attention and consume financial resources. Furthermore, unfavourable orders could have an adverse impact on our business, financial condition, results of operations, and cash flows. We cannot assure you that these legal proceedings or investigations will be decided in our favour and that no further liability will arise out of these proceedings or would not have a material adverse effect on the business, financial condition, results of operations, and cash flows of our Company. Even if we are successful in defending such cases, we may be subject to legal and other costs incurred pursuant to defending such litigation, and such costs may be substantial and not recoverable.
Our Company is in the process of litigating these matters and based on the assessment in accordance with applicable accounting standards, our Company has presently not made provisions of the specific pending legal proceedings (apart from those in relation to tax claims). While our Company has made provision for disputed matters in general, in the event of any adverse rulings in these proceedings or consequent levy of penalties including for amounts beyond the provisions currently made by us, we may need to make payments or make further provisions for future payments, which may increase expenses and current or contingent liabilities. For details of our contingent liabilities, see “  * **Summary of the Draft Offer Document -  Summary of contingent** * * **liabilities** *  ” on page 23, and “  * **-  We have certain contingent liabilities as per Ind AS-37 “Provisions, Contingent** * * **Liabilities, Contingent Assets** *   *”*   * **, and our business, financial condition, results of operations, and cash flows** * * **could be adversely affected if any of these contingent liabilities materialise** *  ” on page 51. Furthermore, there may be certain outstanding matters, as on date or in the future, for which the aforementioned parties may not have been served with summons or relevant case documents, which may result in adverse findings against us. An adverse outcome in any of these proceedings, either individually or in aggregate, may affect our reputation, business, financial condition, results of operations, cash flows and prospects.
* **15.** *    * **We or BSPA may not succeed in continuing to establish, maintain and strengthen the “Bonfiglioli”** * * **brand and the brand could be harmed by complaints and negative publicity, in India and globally,** * * **which could have an adverse impact on our reputation, business, financial condition, results of** * * **operations, and cash flows.** *

Chunk 84
----------------------------------------
## **INTERNAL RISKS**

We depend on BSPA for the use of the “Bonfiglioli” brand, and we share the brand name with other companies within the Bonfiglioli Group. The success of our business depends on our and BSPA’s ability to develop, maintain and strengthen the “Bonfiglioli” brand. To promote the “Bonfiglioli” brand further, we may be required to evolve our marketing practices, which could result in an increase in our costs. Various factors, some of which are beyond our or BSPA’s control, are critical for maintaining and enhancing our brand. These include our or BSPA ability to increase brand awareness among existing and potential customers, adopt new technologies or adapt our systems to customer requirements or emerging industry standards, and protect the intellectual property related to our brand. If we or BSPA fail to preserve the value of the “Bonfiglioli” brand, maintain our reputation, or attract consumers to our brand through, for example, quality after-sale services to our customers, our business could be adversely impacted. Furthermore, our and BSPA’s reputation and the “Bonfiglioli” brand is vulnerable to threats that can be

44
difficult to predict or control, and costly or unfeasible to remediate. In addition, any negative publicity about us, BSPA or the “Bonfiglioli” brand, whether as a result of our own or our third-party service providers’ actual or alleged conduct, complaints or negative reviews by customers, alleged misconduct, unethical business practices, safety breaches or other improper activities, or rumours relating to our or BSPA’s business, directors, officers, employees or shareholders, could harm our reputation, business, financial condition, results of operations, and cash flows. While we have not experienced any such instances of negative publicity in the nine months period ended September 30, 2025 and in Fiscals 2024, 2023 and 2022, any allegations of this nature, even if not proved, may lead to inquiries, investigations, or other legal actions against us which could cause us to incur significant costs to defend ourselves.
The occurrence of any of these instances could create a negative perception of our brand and impact our reputation and operations. Any negative publicity regarding our business partners that we closely cooperate with, or any regulatory inquiries or investigations and lawsuits initiated against them, may also have an impact on the “Bonfiglioli” brand and our or BSPA’s reputation, or subject us to regulatory inquiries or investigations or lawsuits. Our brand image may also be affected negatively if customers believe we are no longer able to offer high-quality gearboxes and drive systems and parts and related services that meet evolving customer demands which, in turn, would affect our sales volumes, business, financial condition, results of operations, and cash flows.

While there are no outstanding regulatory or legal proceedings, no notices, disputes or penalties have arisen or been imposed in connection with these corporate records and other documents as of the date of this Draft Red Herring Prospectus, and accordingly no imminent penalties can be foreseen, we cannot assure you that no notices, dispute or penalties will arise or be imposed on us in this regard in the future.

* **18.** *    * **We require certain approvals and licences in the ordinary course of business, and the failure to obtain** * * **or retain them in a timely manner may adversely affect our operations.** *
Our operations are subject to extensive government regulations, and we are required to obtain and maintain several statutory and regulatory permits, approvals, registrations and licenses under various regulations, guidelines, circulars and statutes regulated by authorities such as the Government of India, the State Governments and certain other regulatory and government authorities, for operating our business such as consent to operate, consent to establish, registration and license to work a factory, no-objection certificate from fire department, amongst others, all of which are required to carry out/ undertake our operations. While we have obtained a number of approvals required for our operations, certain approvals that we have submitted applications for are currently pending. There is no assurance that we will receive these approvals on time or at all. In addition, to operate our manufacturing facilities, we require land use and environmental permits and other operating permits from central, state, and local government entities. While we currently have the necessary approvals to carry out and perform our current plans and operations at our manufacturing facilities, our approvals may expire in the ordinary course, and we may be required to make applications for such renewals. If we fail to obtain or retain any of these approvals or licences, or renewals thereof, in a timely manner, it may disrupt our operations, result in the imposition of penalties and may adversely affect our operations, business, financial condition, results of operations, and cash flows. See “  * **Government and Other Approvals -  Material Approvals pending in respect of our Company** *  ” on page 368. Some of our approvals are valid for a limited duration or are subject to certain conditions. We cannot assure you that these approvals would not be suspended or revoked in the event of, an alleged, non-compliance with any terms or conditions thereof, or pursuant to any regulatory action. If there is any failure by us to comply with the applicable regulations, or if the regulations governing our business are amended, we may incur increased costs, be subject to penalties, have our approvals and permits revoked or suffer a disruption in our operations. See also “  * **Changing laws, rules, and regulations in India and legal uncertainties may adversely affect our business,** * * **financial condition, results of operations, cash flows and prospects** *  ” on page 59. In the future we will be required to apply for fresh approvals and permits for any new manufacturing facilities, including the proposed facility at Talegaon (Maharashtra). For more details, please see “  * **Our ongoing and proposed capacity addition plans are** * * **subject to the risk of unanticipated delays in implementation and cost overruns** *   *.*  ”  on page 40.

## **INTERNAL RISKS**

* **19.** *    * **Non-compliance with, and changes in, safety, health and environmental laws and various labour,** * * **workplace and related laws and regulations may increase our compliance costs and as such adversely** * * **affect our business, financial condition, results of operations, cash flows, and prospects.** *
We are subject to a broad range of safety, health, environmental, labour, workplace and related laws and regulations in the jurisdictions in which we operate, which impose controls on workplace safety, noise emissions, air and water discharges and other aspects of our operations. For further information on the laws and regulations applicable to our business, see “  * **Key Regulations and Policies in India** *  ” on page 219.
The adoption of stricter environmental, labour, health and safety laws and regulations, stricter interpretations of existing laws, increased governmental enforcement of laws or other developments in the future may require that we make additional capital expenditures, incur additional expenses or take other actions in order to remain compliant and maintain our current operations. Compliance with, and changes in, these laws and regulations or

46
terms of approval may increase our compliance costs and adversely affect our business, financial condition, results of operations, cash flows, and prospects. We are also subject to laws and regulations governing relationships with employees in areas such as minimum wage and maximum working hours, overtime, working conditions, hiring and termination of employees, contract labour and work permits.
If there is any failure by us to comply with the applicable regulations or if the regulations governing our business are amended, we may incur increased costs, be subject to penalties, have our approvals and permits revoked or suffer a disruption in our operations, any of which could adversely affect our business. For further information, see “  * **Government and Other Approvals** *  ” on page 366.
* **20.** *    * **Our business depends substantially on the continued efforts of our management including members** * * **of our Senior Management and other qualified personnel, and our operations may be disrupted if we** * * **lose their services.** *
Our success depends substantially on the continued efforts of our management including members of our Senior Management, engineers, technical personnel, and other qualified personnel with expertise in various areas. Our Managing Director as well as certain members of our KMP have been employed at our Company, for more than 15 years, and have contributed significantly to the growth of our business in India. For details of our Managing Director and Senior Management, see “  * **Our Management -  Board of Directors** *  ” and “  * **Our Management -  Our** * * **Key Managerial Personnel** *  ” on pages 230 and 246, respectively. If one or more of our members of the Senior Management or Managing Director or other qualified personnel are unable or unwilling to continue their services with us, we might not be able to replace them easily, in a timely manner, or at all. This in turn could have a material adverse impact on our business, financial condition, results of operations, and cash flows.

If any of our Senior Management other qualified personnel joins a competitor or forms a competing company, we may lose customers, vendor- relationships, know-how and key professionals and staff members. If we are unable to effectively manage our hiring needs or successfully integrate and retain new hires, our efficiency, ability to meet forecasts and productivity levels could suffer, which could adversely affect our business, financial condition, results of operations, and cash flows.

## **INTERNAL RISKS**

47
* **21.** *    * **We use heavy machinery at our manufacturing facilities which could cause bodily harm and accidents,** * * **which in turn could adversely impact our operations.** *
The use of heavy equipment and machinery, which are an integral part of our production process, carries risks such as industrial accidents, bodily harm, fires, explosions, environmental hazards (such as the accidental release of pollutants or hazardous substances) and other unanticipated incidents, each of which may result in disruptions to our business, damage our manufacturing facilities, cause environmental pollution or result in injury and death. While we have not experienced any material injuries or any fatalities at any of our manufacturing facilities in the nine months period ended September 30, 2025 and Fiscals 2024, 2023 and 2022, there can be no assurance that such accidents may not occur in the future, or that we will be able to settle the disputes on time or at all, which will negatively affect our reputation, business, financial condition, results of operations, and cash flows.
* **22.** *    * **Our warranty reserves may be insufficient to cover future warranty claims, which could adversely** * * **affect our business, financial condition, results of operations, and cash flows.** *
We offer warranties to customers when they purchase our products. Under our general terms and conditions of sale and in accordance with industry practice, we typically provide a limited warranty on our products that is usually limited to repair or replacement of defective items or return of, or a credit with respect to, amounts paid for such items. The warranty period for our products is different across the different business units producing them and can range from 12 months to 24 months, and warranty reserves are calculated accordingly. For more details on our warranties, see “  * **Our Business -  Our Operations -  Warranty** *  ” on page 216. The following table shows the warranty provision created as per Ind AS 37 for the period/years indicated:

We are subject to warranty claims under warranty agreements with our customers. Although we have provision for warranties as per Ind AS 37 we may receive warranty claims that exceed our provisions and incur significant expenses. In addition, if there is an increase in our sales volumes in line with our business strategy, we could experience an increase in the number of warranty claims and be required to increase our warranty provisions. These factors in turn could affect our business, financial condition, results of operations, and cash flows.
* **23.** *    * **We may be negatively impacted by any early obsolescence of our manufacturing equipment and the** * * **spare parts or software used in such equipment.** *
We depreciate the cost of our manufacturing equipment and the spare parts or software used in such equipment over their expected useful lives. However, product cycles or manufacturing technology may change periodically, and we may decide to update our gearboxes and drive systems, parts or manufacturing processes more quickly than expected. Moreover, improvements in engineering and manufacturing expertise and efficiency may result in our ability to manufacture our gearboxes and drive systems or parts using less of our currently installed equipment and the spare parts or software used in such equipment. Alternatively, as we ramp and mature the production of our gearboxes and drive systems to higher levels, we may discontinue the use of already installed equipment and the spare parts or software used in such equipment in favour of different or additional equipment, spare part or software. The useful life of any equipment, spare parts or software that would be retired early as a result would be shortened, causing the depreciation on such equipment, spare parts or software to be accelerated, and our business, financial condition, results of operations, and cash flows may be harmed.

48
* **24.** *    * **Disruptions of transportation network and transportation infrastructure or deficiencies in service** * * **provided by our logistic service providers may have an adverse effect on our business, financial** * * **condition, results of operations, and cash flows.** *
We depend on transportation and logistics networks, and the connectivity and conditions of the road, rail, sea and general transportation infrastructure in India and outside in India. Delays in supply and transportation of gearboxes and drive systems to dealers, or customers due to inadequacies of the transportation infrastructure in India, disruptions in railway, road or sea transportation networks due to weather related events, labour strikes, wars or otherwise, could impact our operations. For instance, to overcome supply disruptions on account of the crisis in the Red Sea involving the Houthis in 2023, we stocked higher inventories. While we were able to continue to meet our customer orders on a timely basis due to such inventory management, there is no assurance that such delays will not occur in the future or that we will continue to be able to mitigate the impacts of such disruptions in transportation. We also rely on third parties for transportation services for both domestic sales and exports. Any failure by these service providers to transport our gearboxes and drive systems to dealers on time and in marketable condition or any service deficiency caused by these service providers could have a material impact on our operations. We may also be affected by an increase in fuel costs, as it will have a corresponding impact on freight charges levied by our third party transportation providers that may have an adverse effect on our business, financial condition, results of operations, and cash flows.

Chunk 91
----------------------------------------
## **INTERNAL RISKS**

* **25.** *    * **Our manufacturing operations may be subject to strikes, work stoppages or increased wage demands** * * **by our employees or the employees of our sub-contractors.** *
As of September 30, 2025, we had a workforce of 975 personnel (inclusive of 590 on-roll employees and 385 contract labourers), of which 451 employees were directly engaged by us at our manufacturing sites and 375 employees were engaged as contract labour to carry out our manufacturing operations. The success of our operations depends on the availability of labour and maintaining good relationships with our workforce. Shortage of skilled and unskilled personnel or work stoppages caused by disagreements with employees. This could result in strikes, work stoppages or increased wage demands and could materially and adversely affect our business, financial condition, results of operations, and cash flows.
India has stringent labour legislations that protect the interests of workers. We are also subject to laws and regulations governing relationships with employees, in areas such as minimum wage and maximum working hours, overtime, working conditions, hiring and terminating of employees and work permits. See “  * **Key** * * **Regulations and Policies in India** *  ” on page 219. As of the date of this Draft Red Herring Prospectus, employees at our manufacturing facility at Thirumudivakkam, Tamil Nadu are organised in a union, and we have entered into a 3.5 year wage settlement agreement with effect from October 2025 that prescribes fixed wage increments, bonus payments, leave and holiday entitlements and disciplinary protocols. If our remaining employees similarly unionize, it may become difficult for us to maintain flexible labour policies.
While we have not experienced any disruption in our business operations due to disputes with our workforce in the nine months period ended September 30, 2025 and in Fiscals 2024, 2023 and 2022 there can be no assurance that we will not experience disruption in the future. Such disruptions may adversely affect our business, financial condition, results of operations, and cash flows and may also divert our management’s attention and result in increased costs.
* **26.** *    * **We may be unable to adequately protect our intellectual property rights and those of the Bonfiglioli** * * **Group and an infringement of such intellectual property may substantially harm our business.** *
Our Company does not hold any registered trademarks or patents or designs in its own name and our Company has a non-exclusive, non-transferable right and license to use intellectual property rights of one of our Corporate Promoters, Bonfiglioli S.p.A., including trademarks, patents, and other intellectual property. There can be no assurance that trademarks and patents under our pending applications will be granted or that we will be able to renew our registered trademarks and patents in a timely manner or at all. Any unauthorised or inappropriate use of the Bonfiglioli brand, trademarks and other related intellectual property rights by others in their corporate names, product brands or otherwise could harm our brand image, competitive advantages and business, and dilute or harm our reputation and brand recognition.

Chunk 92
----------------------------------------
## **INTERNAL RISKS**

Despite our efforts to protect our proprietary rights, third parties may attempt to copy or otherwise obtain and use our intellectual property. Although we are not aware of any such infringement in the nine months period ended September 30, 2025 and in Fiscals 2024, 2023 and 2022, monitoring unauthorised use of our intellectual property is difficult and costly, and we cannot assure you that the steps we have taken or will take will prevent

49
misappropriation of our intellectual property. From time to time, we may have to resort to litigation to enforce our intellectual property rights, which could result in substantial costs and diversion of our resources.
In addition, our Company uses the Bonfiglioli brand and certain intellectual property owned by the Bonfiglioli Group pursuant to licenses granted to us for specific purposes, including under the License Agreement. While we have implemented measures to protect such intellectual property in dealings with third parties such as our dealer network and supplier base, our efforts to protect the intellectual property and other proprietary information of the Bonfiglioli Group may prove to be inadequate and there could be leakages through our employees, dealers, and vendors of the Bonfiglioli Group’s intellectual property. While we have not experienced such instances in the nine months period ended September 30, 2025 and in Fiscals 2024, 2023 and 2022, we may be required to indemnify BSPA for any losses arising out of our failure to ensure adequate protection of BSPA’s intellectual property in the future. Any such instance may result in us defending such claims, which may involve being subject to legal and other costs which may be substantial and not recoverable, thereby adversely affecting our business, financial condition, results of operations, and cash flows.
* **27.** *    * **We require power, fuel and water for our operations and any disruption to the supply of power, fuel** * * **or water could disrupt our manufacturing operations and increase our production costs, which could** * * **adversely affect our results of operations.** *
Adequate and uninterrupted supply of electrical power and water is critical to our operations. Although we maintain power back-ups through generators at each of our manufacturing facilities, we may experience disruptions in power supply due to natural calamities or scarcity of fuel supply at our manufacturing facilities from time to time. Any such disruption may lead us to use our back-up generators and prolonged use of such generators may increase our production costs. Significant and prolonged disruptions in the availability of power and fuel could require us to suspend our operations. We may also be affected by an increase in power and fuel costs that we may not be able to adequately pass on to our customers. Any significant increase in the cost of electricity and fuel could lead to an increase in production cost and adversely affect our profitability and operating margins. Operations at our manufacturing facilities also depend on a steady supply of water. While we have not faced disruptions in supply of water at our manufacturing facilities in the nine months period ended September 30, 2025 and Fiscals 2024, 2023 and 2022, an insufficient supply of water any time in the future may result in a need to limit or delay or suspend our operations, which could adversely affect our business, financial condition, results of operations, and cash flows.

Chunk 93
----------------------------------------
## **INTERNAL RISKS**

* **28.** *    * **We rely primarily on third-party insurance policies to insure our operations-related risks. If our** * * **insurance coverage is inadequate, it may have an adverse effect on our business, financial condition,** * * **results of operations, and cash flows.** *
We maintain insurance which we believe is standard in our industry in India and in amounts which we believe to be commercially appropriate for a variety of risks, including insurance policies related to fire, natural disasters such as cyclones, earthquakes, burglary, theft, external accidental damage, terrorism, breakdowns (electronic or mechanical) of electronics, as required by applicable law and to protect our assets and operations and also in respect of our employees such as health and life insurance. The table below sets forth our insurance cover as a percentage of total assets for the period/years indicated:

**As at December 31, 2024**
**As at December 31, 2023**

5,766.83 5,597.17 5,811.15 4,303.65
Amount of insurance coverage* 11,121.72 10,221.85 8,465.08 7,974.28 Insurance coverage as a percentage to Tangible assets comprising of property, plant and equipment (excluding freehold land & leasehold improvements), Capital Work in Progress and inventories
192.86% 182.63% 145.67% 185.29%
** The Company’s insurance policies are on reinstated value basis*
However, such insurance may not be adequate to cover all losses or liabilities that may arise from our operations, particularly when the loss suffered is not easily quantifiable. In addition, while the majority of our contracts with suppliers include indemnification provisions for liabilities arising from product defects, such recourses are subject to limitations set for the contracts, and there is no assurance that we will be able to claim losses from suppliers

50
due to product liability claims or recalls resulting for defective supplies.  Our insurance policies also contain exclusions and limitations on coverage, and, accordingly, we may not be able to assert claims for the full amount of any liability or losses. While we have not experienced any uninsured losses or liabilities in the nine months period ended September 30, 2025 and in Fiscals 2024, 2023 and 2022, we may incur losses for which we are not insured in the future as such risks may either be uninsurable or not insurable on commercially acceptable terms.

Chunk 94
----------------------------------------
## **INTERNAL RISKS**

Even if our insurance coverage is adequate to cover our direct losses, we may not be able to take remedial actions or other appropriate measures in a timely manner or at all. Furthermore, our claim records may affect the premiums which insurance companies may charge us in the future. Furthermore, there can be no assurance that in the future we will be able to maintain insurance of the types or at levels which we deem necessary or adequate or at premiums which we deem to be commercially acceptable. If we are unable to pass the effects of increased insurance costs on to our customers, the costs of higher insurance premiums could have a material adverse effect on our costs and profitability. Furthermore, our insurance coverage expires from time to time. We apply for the renewal of our insurance coverage in the normal course of our business. While certain of our insurance policies are due for renewal as of date, we cannot assure you that such renewals will be granted in a timely manner, at acceptable cost or at all. Additionally, some of our insurance claims may be rejected by the insurance companies in the future and there can be no assurance that any claim under the insurance policies maintained by us will be honoured fully, in part, or on time. The occurrence of an event for which we are not insured, where the loss is more than the insured limits or where we are unable to successfully assert insurance claims from losses, it could result in uninsured liabilities. Any uninsured losses or liabilities could result in an adverse effect on our business, financial condition, results of operations, and cash flows.
* **29.** *    * **Our manufacturing facilities at Talegaon (Maharashtra) and Mannur (Tamil Nadu) are not located** * * **on land owned by us and we have only leasehold rights. In the event we lose or are unable to renew** * * **such leasehold rights, our business, financial condition, results of operations and cash flows may be** * * **adversely affected.** *
As on September 30, 2025, two of our operational manufacturing facilities are located on land that is not owned by us, and the land on which we propose to set up a new manufacturing facility to manufacture heavy-duty gear motors is not owned by us. The table below provides information on properties held by us on a leasehold basis as of the date of this Draft Red Herring Prospectus:

----------------------------------------
## **INTERNAL RISKS**

**S. No. Facility Address Lease/Freehold Date of expiry of agreement 1.**    Mannur, Tamil Nadu
Survey No. 528/1, 528/3, 528/4, 528/5A, 528/5B and 528/5C, Mannur Village, Sriperumbudur Taluk, Chennai -  602105, Tamil Nadu, India
Leased July 31, 2026
**2.**    Talegaon, Maharashtra

We occupy the premises for our Mannur Facility on a leasehold basis based on a lease agreement that was entered into for a period of 14 months with effect from June 1, 2025, and that may be terminated by either party with at least six months’ notice. The premises for our manufacturing facility operational at Talegaon is owned by Maharashtra Industrial Development Corporation (“  **MIDC**  ”) and has been leased to us pursuant to a lease agreement dated March 24, 2025 for a period of 95 years with effect from May 1, 2022.  We are obligated to comply with certain covenants under the agreement, including restrictions on construction on the land, planting and maintaining trees along the periphery of the property and preferentially employing labourers who fulfil MIDC’s criteria or are locals from the area near the facility.
In the event that these existing leases are terminated or they are not renewed on commercially acceptable terms, we may suffer a disruption in our operations. If alternative premises are not available at the same or similar costs, sizes or locations, our business, financial condition, results of operations and cash flows may be adversely affected. If we are unable to comply with the conditions of our lease arrangements, we may not be able to maintain use of such premises and may have to relocate our operations which may not be cost-effective. In addition, any regulatory non-compliance by the landlords or adverse development relating to the landlords’ title or ownership rights to such properties, including as a result of any non-compliance by the landlords, may entail significant disruptions to our operations and expose us to reputational risks, especially if we are forced to vacate leased spaces following any such developments. If our sales do not increase in line with our rent and costs, including setup and interior design costs, our profitability, business, financial condition, results of operations, and cash flows could be adversely affected. While we have not faced any instances of difficulties in negotiating our lease arrangements or

Chunk 96
----------------------------------------

51
premature termination of existing lease agreements that led to any adverse effect on our business or operations in the nine months period ended September 30, 2025 and in Fiscals 2024, 2023 and 2022, we cannot assure you that such instances will not occur in the future.

We have disclosed certain contingent liabilities as per Ind AS-37 “  *Provisions, Contingent Liabilities and* *Contingent Assets*  ”, derived from our Restated Financial Statements. *Disputed income-tax demands pending before various forums includes demand orders received under Income Tax Act,1961 with respect to* *certain disallowances of expenditure for AY 04-05 (proceedings pending before the High Court), AY 21-22 (proceedings pending with CIT-* *Appeals) and AY 22-23 (demand order received from Assessing Officer) amounting to ₹43.38 million (December 31, 2024 - ₹32.40 million,* *December 31, 2023 - ₹17.10 million, and December 31, 2022 - ₹17.10 million) and downward transfer pricing adjustments relating to certain* *corporate support charges for AY 15-16, 17-18, 18-19, 20-21 and 23-24 amounting to ₹36.54 million (December 31, 2024 - ₹54.13 million,* *December 31, 2023 - Nil, December 31, 2022 - Nil).*

Our future contingent liabilities may crystallize and become actual liabilities. If any of our future contingent liabilities become actual liabilities, our business, financial condition, results of operations, and cash flows may be adversely affected. Chunk 97
----------------------------------------
## **INTERNAL RISKS**

* **31.** *    * **Our inability to collect receivables and default in payment from our customers could adversely affect** * * **our cash flows.** *
We are exposed to counterparty credit risk in the usual course of our business due to the nature of, and the inherent risks involved in, dealings and arrangements with customers who may delay or fail to make payments or perform other contractual obligations. The majority of our sales are on an open credit basis, with standard payment terms of generally up to 240 days. While we generally monitor the ability of our customers to pay these open credit arrangements and limit the credit we extend to what we believe is reasonable based on an evaluation of customer’s financial condition and payment history, there is a risk that our estimates may not be accurate. As a result, we may still experience losses because of a client’s inability to honour their commitments towards us.
The table below sets forth our trade receivables as of the dates stated:
**Particulars As at September 30, 2025**
**As at December 31, 2024**
**As at December 31, 2023**
**As at December 31, 2022** * **(₹ million)** * Trade receivables 4,787.26 4,095.62 4,103.34 3,767.40

If we are unable to collect receivables or if our customers request modifications to their payment arrangements, it could increase our receivables or affect our working capital requirements. While we have had to write-off some receivables in the past that did not have a material impact on our results of operations in the nine months period ended September 30, 2025, and in Fiscals 2024, 2023 and 2022, any lapse in honouring significant amounts/ receivables in the future may lead to us writing-off bad debts. An increase in bad debts or in defaults by our customer, may compel us to utilize greater amounts of our operating working capital and result in increased interest costs, thereby adversely affecting our business, results of operations, cash flows and financial condition.

52
* **32.** *    * **If we are unable to establish and maintain an effective internal controls and compliance system, our** * * **business and reputation could be adversely affected.** *
We are responsible for establishing and maintaining a system of internal controls over financial reporting and operations that is appropriate for the scale and complexity of our business. However, any system of internal control, regardless of how well it is designed and implemented, has inherent limitations. These include the possibility of human error, lapses in judgment, or the failure of internal processes and systems to address evolving operational risks. We periodically test and update our internal processes and systems and there have been no past instances in the nine months period ended September 30, 2025, and Fiscals 2024, 2023 and 2022 of failure to maintain effective internal controls and compliance system. However, we are exposed to operational risks arising from the potential inadequacy or failure of internal processes or systems, and our actions may not be sufficient to ensure effective internal checks and balances in all circumstances.

Chunk 98
----------------------------------------
## **INTERNAL RISKS**

Furthermore, our operations are subject to anti-corruption laws and regulations. These laws prohibit us and our employees and intermediaries from bribing, being bribed or making other prohibited payments to government officials or other persons to obtain or retain business or gain some other business advantage. We participate in collaborations and relationships with third parties whose actions could potentially subject us to liability under these laws or other local anti-corruption laws. While our code of conduct requires our employees and intermediaries to comply with all applicable laws , and we continue to enhance our policies and procedures in an effort to ensure compliance with applicable anti-corruption laws and regulations, these measures may not prevent the breach of such anti-corruption laws, as there are risks of such breaches in emerging markets, such as India, including within the manufacturing sector. If we are not in compliance with applicable anti-corruption laws, we may be subject to criminal and civil penalties, disgorgement and other sanctions and remedial measures, and legal expenses, which could have an adverse impact on our business, financial condition, results of operations, cash flows and liquidity. Likewise, any investigation of any potential violations of anti-corruption laws by the relevant authorities could also have an adverse impact on our business and reputation.
A significant failure in our internal controls could result in our inability to produce reliable financial reports or prevent fraud. This could lead to a loss of investor confidence in our company and a decline in the market price of our Equity Shares, which would harm our business and reputation. While our internal audit function continually evaluates our processes and we have not identified any material weaknesses in our internal controls in the nine months period ended September 30, 2025 and Fiscal 2024, 2023 and 2022, we cannot provide any assurance that such weaknesses will not be identified or that control failures will not occur in the future.
* **33.** *    * **We have used information from the F&S Report which has been commissioned and paid for by our** * * **Company for industry related data in this Draft Red Herring Prospectus and any reliance on such** * * **information is subject to inherent risks.** *
We have used the information from the F&S Report for industry related data in this Draft Red Herring Prospectus, which has been commissioned and paid for by our Company, pursuant to an engagement letter dated October 16, 2025. The F&S Report has been prepared and issued by Frost & Sullivan for the purpose of understanding the industry, exclusively for the purpose of this Offer. The information is subject to various limitations, highlights certain industry and market data relating to us and our competitors which may not be based on any standard methodology and is based upon certain assumptions that are subjective in nature. Accordingly, investors should read the industry related disclosures in this Draft Red Herring Prospectus. Neither our Company, nor the BRLMs are related to Frost & Sullivan. The F&S Report may use certain methodologies for market sizing and forecasting and may include numbers relating to our Company that differ from those we record internally. Industry sources and publications are also prepared based on information as of specific dates and may no longer be current or reflect current trends. Industry sources and publications may also base their information on estimates, projections, forecasts and assumptions that may prove to be incorrect. Statements from third parties that involve estimates are subject to change, and actual amounts may differ materially from those included in this Draft Red Herring Prospectus. Accordingly, investors should not place undue reliance on, or base their investment decision solely on this information. For further details, see “  * **Certain Conventions, Presentation of Financial, Industry and Market** * * **Data -  Industry and Market Data** *  ” on page 16.

Chunk 99
----------------------------------------
## **INTERNAL RISKS**

* **34.** *    * **We have not been able to obtain certain records of educational qualifications of one of our Individual** * * **Promoters.** *
One of our Individual Promoters, Luisa Lusardi, aged 92, has been unable to trace copies of documents pertaining to her educational qualification from the Elisabetta Sirani Institute in Bologna, Italy. While she has written to the relevant organisation seeking copies of the certification/attestation of her diploma from Elisabetta Sirani Institute,

53
she has received a response from the institute stating that following a search of their historical archives there are no general class or exam registers dating back to 1950-1953. The details of her education qualifications have not been included in this Draft Red Herring Prospectus and for further details of Luisa Lusardi, please see “  * **Promoter** * * **and Promoter Group -  Details of our Individual Promoters** *  ” on page 250. Further, there can be no assurances that such Individual Promoter will be able to trace the relevant documents pertaining to her educational qualifications in future, or at all.
* **35.** *    * **Internal or external fraud or misconduct by our employees could adversely affect our reputation,** * * **business, financial condition, results of operations, and cash flows.** *
We may be subject to instances of fraud, misappropriation, unauthorised acts and misconduct by our representatives and employees or any third parties employed by us which may go unnoticed for certain periods of time before corrective action is taken. While we have not been subject to such instances in the nine months period ended September 30, 2025 and in Fiscals 2024, 2023 and 2022, fraudulent and unauthorised conduct by our employees could also bind us to transactions that exceed the scope of authorisation and present significant risks to us. As a result, we may be subject to regulatory sanctions, brand and reputational damage or financial harm. It is not always possible to deter fraud or misconduct by employees and the precautions we take and the systems we have put in place to prevent and deter such activities may not be effective in all cases. Further, since we employ third parties for certain operations, we are exposed to the risk of theft and embezzlement. In addition, we may be subject to regulatory or other proceedings in connection with such acts by our employees, which could adversely affect our goodwill. Such instances could also adversely affect our reputation, brand, business, financial condition, results of operations, and cash flows. Even when we identify such instances and pursue legal recourse or file claims with our insurance carriers, we cannot assure you that we will recover any amounts lost through such instances of fraud, misappropriation, unauthorised acts and misconduct by our representatives and employees.

Chunk 100
----------------------------------------
## **INTERNAL RISKS**

* **36.** *    * **Technology failures could disrupt our operations and adversely affect our business operations and** * * **financial performance.** *
IT systems are critical to our ability to manage our large manufacturing operations, including in terms of carrying out payroll processing and travel management functions, and implementing our manufacturing excellence system. If we do not allocate and effectively manage the resources necessary to build and sustain the proper IT infrastructure, we could be subject to disruptions in our key day to day operations.
We have also introduced IoT enabled software for our yaw and pitch drives. We are therefore exposed to challenges relating to the building of new IT structures which can subject us to certain errors, inefficiencies, disruptions and, in some instances, poor customer service. Our IT systems, and the systems of our third-party IT service providers may also be vulnerable to a variety of interruptions due to events beyond our control, including, but not limited to, natural disasters, terrorist attacks, telecommunications failures, computer viruses, hackers and other security issues. While we have not experienced any such technology failure that has resulted in any significant disruption in our operation in the nine months period ended September 30, 2025 and in Fiscals 2024, 2023 and 2022, there can be no assurance that such events will not occur in the future. Although we have security initiatives and disaster recovery plans in place to mitigate its risk to these vulnerabilities, such measures may not have been effectively implemented or may not be adequate to ensure that our operations are not disrupted.
* **37.** *    * **A majority of our Directors do not have prior experience of holding a directorship in a company listed** * * **on the Stock Exchanges.** *
A majority of our Directors do not have any prior experience of holding directorship in a company listed on the Stock Exchanges. Post listing of the Equity Shares, our Company and our Directors will be subject to the applicable regulatory requirements, including the requirements prescribed under SEBI Listing Regulations. Upon listing of the Equity Shares, the responsibilities of our Directors  *inter alia*  include monitoring the effectiveness of our Company’s governance practices and suggesting changes and overseeing our financial reporting systems along with the disclosures and communication made by our Company. We cannot assure you that we will be able to comply with the applicable regulatory requirements at all times. Any non-compliance with the regulatory framework, due to lack of experience or otherwise, may subject us to adverse regulatory actions, and have an impact on the listing price of our Equity Shares.

Chunk 101
----------------------------------------
## **INTERNAL RISKS**

* **38.** *    * **The withdrawal, cessation, reversal of benefits and exemptions availed by our Company could have a** * * **material adverse effect on our business, operations, prospects or financial results.** *

54
We currently avail benefits under certain schemes such as the Remission of Duties and Taxes on Exported Products (“  **RoDTEP**  ”) scheme, export promotion capital goods (“  **EPCG**  ”) scheme and duty drawbacks. As a result, we are able to import certain materials at concessional rates of duty and in some instances, free of duty, and avail certain benefits for products that we export from our facilities. We also benefit from certain free trade agreements between India and Asian countries from where we import electric motors and hydraulic motors. Any reduction or withdrawal of such benefits or our inability to meet any of the conditions prescribed under any of the schemes would adversely affect our business, financial condition, results of operations, and cash flows. Furthermore, the benefits/ incentives under such schemes are available to us for a fixed period subject to compliance with various terms and conditions and such incentive are not subject to renewal. There can be no assurance that we will continue to enjoy these benefits in the future or will be able to obtain timely disbursement of such benefits
* **39.** *    * **Information relating to the available capacity and capacity utilization of our manufacturing facilities** * * **included in this Draft Red Herring Prospectus are based on various assumptions and estimates and** * * **future production and capacity may vary.** *
Information relating to the available capacity and capacity utilization of our manufacturing facilities included in this Draft Red Herring Prospectus, specifically under “  * **Our Business -  Our Operations -  Manufacturing** * * **Facilities** *  ” on page 212, are based on various assumptions and estimates of our management that have been taken into account by Prabhakar Prabhu, independent chartered engineer, in his certificate dated February 8, 2026, in the calculation of the available capacity, utilization and utilization as a percentage of available capacity at our manufacturing facilities and such calculations may not be computed on the basis of, or in accordance with, any standard methodology and may not be comparable to that employed by our competitors. These assumptions and estimates include the number of machine-working hours per day adjusted for deductions on account of shift changes, number of shifts, and seamless working of each machine cycle time.

Chunk 102
----------------------------------------
## **INTERNAL RISKS**

Future capacity utilization rates may vary from the estimated production capacities of our manufacturing facilities and their historical capacity utilization rates. Undue reliance should therefore not be placed on the historical available capacity and capacity utilization for our facilities included in this Draft Red Herring Prospectus.
* **40.** *    * **Our Promoters may have interest other than reimbursement of expenses incurred and normal** * * **remuneration or benefits.** *
Our Promoters are interested in our Company, in addition to regular remuneration or benefits and reimbursement of expenses and such interests are to the extent of dividends, to the extent (i) that they have promoted our Company; (ii) of transactions entered into by our Company with them; (iii) of their direct and indirect shareholding in our Company; (iv) of the dividend payable, if any, and any other distributions in respect of the Equity Shares held by one of our Corporate Promoters, from time to time; and (v) agreements entered amongst our Company and our Promoters and payments made pursuant to such agreements. For payments that are made by our Company to related parties, see  * **“Summary of the Draft Offer Document -  Summary of Related Party Transactions** *  ” on page 23.
For details of interests of our Directors and Promoter, see “  * **Our Management -  Interests of Directors** *  ” page 235 and “  * **Our Promoter and Promoter Group -  Interests of our Promoters** *  ” on page 253.
* **41.** *    * **Certain of our Promoters and Directors may be involved with ventures which are in the same line of** * * **business as our Company. This may involve conflict of interests, which could adversely impact our** * * **business.** *
Certain of our Promoters and Directors, namely Bonfiglioli S.p.A. (Promoter), BON-FI S.r.l. (Promoter), Sonia Bonfiglioli (Promoter and Director), Luciano Bonfiglioli (Promoter), Luisa Lusardi (Promoter), Ravindra Pisharody (Chairperson and Independent Director), Kennady V Kaippally (Managing Director), Gatham Anadhan Balaji (Whole-Time Director and Chief Financial Officer) Massimo Sarti (Non-Executive and Non-Independent Director) and Marco Mossuto (Non-Executive and Non-Independent Director), may be involved with ventures which are in the same line of business as our Company. While exercising their duties as the Directors and rights as our shareholder, the Promoters and Directors may consider the interest of all ventures in which they are involved, which may not align with our interests. We cannot assure you that such Promoters and Directors, will not favour the interests of such other ventures or companies over our interests or that we will be able to suitably resolve any such conflict without an adverse effect on our business or operations. This in turn may give rise to various conflicts of interest between us and such Promoters and Directors, which could impact our business or operations. We will adopt the necessary procedures and practices as permitted by law and regulatory guidelines to address any conflict situations as and when they arise. For details of risks related to related party transactions,

55
see “ -  * **We have entered into and will continue to enter into related party transactions with BSPA and companies** * * **within the Bonfiglioli Group that may involve conflicts of interest, which could adversely impact our business** *  ” on page 35 below. * **42.** *    * **Our Statutory Auditor’s examination report on our restated financial statements discloses certain** * * **modifications and certain matters specified in the Companies (Audit and Auditors) Rules, 2014 and** * * **Companies Auditor’s Report Order, 2020, that were included in the reports for the audited financial** * * **statements for the nine months period ended September 30, 2025, and Fiscals 2024, 2023 and 2022.** *
Our Statutory Auditor’s report on the audited financial statements for Fiscals 2024, 2023 and 2022, under the section legal and regulatory requirements included a modification which indicated that the daily back-up of the books of account and other relevant books and papers, in electronic mode, has not been kept on servers physically located in India. In addition, our Statutory Auditor has also made certain modification in its report on the audited financial statements for Fiscal 2024 with respect to the accounting software as well as third-party accounting software used by us for maintaining our books of account. These are: (i) that the feature of recording audit trail (edit log) facility was not enabled in the accounting software for certain tables and fields and for certain changes being made when using privileged/administrative access rights; and (ii) that the third party accounting software’s feature of recording audit trail (edit log) facility, was not operated throughout the year at the application and database layer. As a result, our Statutory Auditor was unable to comment upon whether there was any instance of audit trail feature being tampered with, where enabled, throughout the year in respect of the accounting software as well as third-party accounting software.
In addition, our Statutory Auditor in the auditor’s report to the audited financial statements for Fiscals 2024, 2023 and 2022, issued under the Companies Auditor’s Report Order, 2020 (CARO) included a modification which indicated that there have been delays up to 60 days of tax deducted at source, 79 days in case of professional tax and 328 days of tax collected at source in Fiscal 2024, slight delays of tax deducted at source in Fiscal 2023 and slight delays of tax deducted at source, professional tax and provident fund contributions in Fiscal 2022.

Chunk 104
----------------------------------------
## **INTERNAL RISKS**

While these modifications did not result in any adjustments being made to the Restated Financial Information, there can be no assurance that the audit reports for any future fiscal periods will not contain modifications that may require significant adjustments. Investors should consider these observations of our Statutory Auditor in evaluating our business, financial condition, results of operations, and cash flows.
* **43.** *    * **There have been delays in payment of statutory dues by our Company. For further information on the statutory dues payable by us, see “  * **Key Regulations and Policies in India** *  ” on page 219.

We cannot assure you that going forward we will be able to make payment of our statutory dues in a timely manner or at all, which could result in penal or other regulatory action including payment of interest on the delay in payment of statutory dues, which could adversely affect our business, financial condition, results of operations, and cash flows.
* **44.** *    * **We depend on contractors and sub-contractors for the timely and successful completion of** * * **construction works, and their failure to complete the project on time or at all may adversely affect our** * * **business and profitability.** *
We may engage contractors to perform some of our construction work at our proposed facility in Talegaon (Maharashtra). Any delays by our contractors to complete the construction of a project could result in our inability to meet project timelines. Our contractors may not be able to replace sub-contractors without any disruptions or adverse consequences to our operations and in certain situations, may be compelled to engage certain sub- contractors over multiple projects to derive cost efficiencies. There can be no assurance that the sub-contractors will continue to perform their obligations to the same standards as those required by us or that our contractors will be able to enter into new or continue our existing arrangements with sub-contractors on acceptable terms, which could have an adverse effect on our manufacturing costs and schedules, which may impact our business and profitability. Further, our sub-contractors may fail to comply with applicable laws including labour laws, regulations and governance requirements. While our sub-contractors have not failed to operate in compliance with applicable laws in the nine months period ended September 30, 2025 and in Fiscals 2024, 2023 and 2022, we may be held accountable for the consequences of non-compliance by them in the future, which may result in fines, penalties or other regulatory actions, leading to disruption of operations and impact on financial performance.

* **45.** *    * **We have in this Draft Red Herring Prospectus included certain non-generally accepted accounting** * * **principle (“Non-GAAP”) financial measures related to our operations and financial performance.** * * **These Non-GAAP measures may vary from any standard methodology that is applicable across the** * * **industry in which we operate, and therefore may not be comparable with financial or statistical** * * **information of similar nomenclature computed and presented by other companies.** *
Certain Non-GAAP financial measures relating to our operations and financial performance such as EBITDA, EBITDA (excluding Other Income) and EBITDA (excluding Other Income) Margin, PAT Margin, Net Debt,

57
Return on Capital Employed and Return on Net Worth have been included in this Draft Red Herring Prospectus. We compute and disclose such Non-GAAP financial measures and statistical information relating to our operations and financial performance as we consider such information to be useful measures of our business and financial performance, and because such measures are frequently used by securities analysts, investors and others to evaluate the operational performance of other manufacturing companies, many of which provide such Non-GAAP financial measures and other industry related statistical and operational information. These Non-GAAP measures are a supplemental measure of our performance and liquidity that is not required by, or presented in accordance with, Ind AS, Indian GAAP, IFRS or U.S. GAAP. Further, these Non-GAAP measures are not a measurement of our financial performance or liquidity under Ind AS, Indian GAAP, IFRS or U.S. GAAP and should not be considered in isolation or construed as an alternative to cash flows, profit/ (loss) for the years/ period or any other measure of financial performance or as an indicator of our operating performance, liquidity, profitability or cash flows generated by operating, investing or financing activities derived in accordance with Ind AS, Indian GAAP, IFRS or U.S. GAAP.
These Non-GAAP Measures and such other industry related statistical and other information relating to our operations and financial performance may not be computed on the basis of any standard methodology that is applicable across the industry and are not measures of operating performance or liquidity defined by generally accepted accounting principles, and therefore may not be comparable to financial measures and industry related statistical information of similar nomenclature that may be computed and presented by other manufacturing companies. For further information, see “  * **Other Financial Information -  Non-GAAP Measures** *  ” on page 317.

* **48.** *    * **After the completion of the Offer, one of our Corporate Promoters, Bonfiglioli S.p.A will continue to** * * **hold substantial shareholding in our Company.** *
As on the date of this Draft Red Herring Prospectus, one of our Corporate Promoters, Bonfiglioli S.p.A. holds 100% of the share capital of our Company including shares held by six nominees, on behalf of the BSPA. For details of their shareholding pre and post Offer, see “  * **Capital Structure** *  ” on page 85. 58
significant influence over our business policies and affairs and all matters requiring Shareholders’ approval, including the composition of our Board, the adoption of amendments to our articles of association, the approval of mergers, strategic acquisitions or joint ventures or the sales of substantially all of our assets, and the policies for dividends, lending, investments and capital expenditures. This concentration of ownership also may delay, defer or even prevent a change in control of our Company and may make some transactions more difficult or impossible without the support of our shareholders. The interests of the BSPA as our controlling shareholder could conflict with our interests or the interests of its shareholders. * **49.** *    * **The requirements of being a listed company may strain our resources which may have a material** * * **adverse impact on our operations.** *
The requirements of being a listed company may strain our resources. As a listed company, we will incur significant legal, accounting, corporate governance, and other expenses that we did not incur as an unlisted company. We will be subject to the SEBI Listing Regulations which will require us to file audited annual and unaudited quarterly reports with respect to our business, financial condition, results of operations, and cash flows. If we experience any delays, we may fail to satisfy our reporting obligations and/or we may not be able to readily determine and accordingly report any changes in our business, financial condition, results of operations, and cash flows as promptly as other listed companies. Furthermore, as a listed company, we will need to maintain and improve the effectiveness of our disclosure controls and procedures and internal control over financial reporting, including keeping adequate records of daily transactions. In order to maintain and improve the effectiveness of our disclosure controls and procedures and internal control over financial reporting, significant resources and management attention will be required. As a result, our management’s attention may be diverted from our business concerns, which may adversely affect our business, financial condition, results of operations, cash flows, and prospects. If we fail to effectively implement sufficient disclosure controls and procedures and internal control procedures over financial reporting, we may be unable to successfully manage or accurately detect and report our future financial risks. In addition, we may need to hire additional legal and accounting staff with appropriate experience and technical accounting knowledge, but we cannot assure you that we will be able to do so in a timely manner or at all.
